mission.title: Install and Configure Metal3 Io on Kubernetes
mission.description: The Bare Metal Operator provides a Kubernetes API for managing bare metal hosts, enabling users to provision and manage physical servers as if they were virtual machines. Installing this operator allows you to integrate bare metal provisioning into your Kubernetes environment seamlessly.

steps.title: Create a Namespace
steps.description:
First, create a namespace for the Bare Metal Operator to run in. Run the following command to create the namespace `metal3`:
```bash
kubectl create namespace metal3
```

steps.title: Deploy the Bare Metal Operator
steps.description:
Next, apply the manifests for the Bare Metal Operator. Use the following command to deploy the operator from the GitHub repository:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/metal3-io/baremetal-operator/v1.10.0/deploy/baremetal-operator.yaml -n metal3
```

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description:
After deploying the operator, verify that the pods are running correctly. Use the following command:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n metal3
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the Bare Metal Operator pods running in the 'metal3' namespace and the RBAC configuration applied correctly. You can also verify the logs to ensure there are no errors.

uninstall.title: Remove the Bare Metal Operator
uninstall.description:
To uninstall the Bare Metal Operator, delete the resources using the following command:
```bash
kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/metal3-io/baremetal-operator/v1.10.0/deploy/baremetal-operator.yaml -n metal3
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Bare Metal Operator
upgrade.description:
To upgrade the Bare Metal Operator, apply the new version of the manifest. For example, to upgrade to version 1.11.0, run:
```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/metal3-io/baremetal-operator/v1.11.0/deploy/baremetal-operator.yaml -n metal3
```

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Running
troubleshooting.description:
If the Bare Metal Operator pods are not running, check the pod logs for errors using:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n metal3
```

troubleshooting.title: Resource Quotas
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure that your cluster has enough resources (CPU, Memory) to run the Bare Metal Operator. Check the resource usage with:
```bash
kubectl top nodes
```

troubleshooting.title: Namespace Issues
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter issues with resources not being found, ensure that you are operating in the correct namespace (`metal3`). You can switch namespaces with:
```bash
kubectl config set-context --current --namespace=metal3
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to interact with your cluster.
